chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Necessitates Discretionary Punishment, Legal Punishment, Stoning, Execution, and Exile in Adultery

text: : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَزْنِي وَ لَمْ يَدْخُلْ بِأَهْلِهِ أَ يُحْصَنُ قَالَ "لاَ وَ لاَ بِالْأَمَةِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5039 - In the narration of Aṣim from Muhammad ibn Muslim, he said:
I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man who commits adultery but has not consummated marriage with his wife. Is he considered muhsan (legally married and liable for stoning)?
Imam (as) replied: "No, and not with a bondwoman (amah) either."
